exampleSentences: Anh muốn mua một quả táo nguyên cho bữa sáng.
Chị ấy để nguyên chiếc bánh trên bàn mà không cắt ra.
Em thích ăn nguyên trái cam hơn là ép thành nước.
notes:
nguyên means whole, entire, intact — not cut, not divided, not changed.  

It’s an adjective and usually comes after the noun: something is complete in one piece or in its full state. It can also mean “unchanged” when talking about condition or situation.  

Very common in fixed phrases like còn nguyên (still intact) and giữ nguyên (keep the same).  

Close ideas: trọn (whole, complete, often more emotional), toàn (all, entire area/amount). Opposite idea: một phần, nửa.

definitions.vi: Toàn bộ, nguyên vẹn hoặc không thay đổi.

example.vi: Tôi muốn ăn nguyên quả táo.
example.en: I want to eat a whole apple.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I want to buy a whole apple for breakfast.
She left the whole cake on the table without cutting it.
I like to eat a whole orange rather than squeeze it into juice.
notes_md:
**nguyên** means *whole, entire, intact* — not cut, not divided, not changed.  

It’s an adjective and usually comes **after** the noun: something is complete in one piece or in its full state. It can also mean “unchanged” when talking about condition or situation.  

Very common in fixed phrases like **còn nguyên** (still intact) and **giữ nguyên** (keep the same).  

Close ideas: **trọn** (whole, complete, often more emotional), **toàn** (all, entire area/amount). Opposite idea: **một phần**, **nửa**.
